framework.name: Capability-Identity Gap Framework
framework.description: Zhou argues existing 'boring infra' (OAuth, X.509, SPIFFE) fails because none detects when an agent's capabilities change post-authorization. Proposes novel composition: X.509 v3 extended with skills manifest hashes, reproducibility commitments, and hash-linked interaction ledgers. Critically, claims 'no existing framework translates governance aspirations into enforceable cryptographic infrastructure' — directly contradicting the 'just compose OAuth+JWT' consensus.
framework.raw_quote: Our contribution is not in novel cryptographic primitives; rather, we show that standard mechanisms—X.509 certificates, SHA-256 hashing, Ed25519 signatures, hash chains—when composed through the capability-context separation, are sufficient to enforce G1–G3 with negligible overhead

source.title: Cryptographic Binding and Reproducibility Verification for AI Agent ...
